title: Ctenitis ampla
intro: Ctenitis ampla, also known as the broad-leaved ctenitis, is a species of fern belonging to the family Dryopteridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antidiabetic properties. Ctenitis ampla is rich in bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and it is also used in the form of dried leaves or extracts to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Ctenitis ampla is classified as a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Ctenitis, which is a group of ferns that are native to tropical and subtropical regions. The family Dryopteridaceae is a large and diverse group of ferns that are found worldwide, with many species adapted to different environments. Ctenitis ampla is a specific species within this family and is known for its unique characteristics and growth habits. This classification helps to identify and distinguish Ctenitis ampla from other species within the same family.
uses: Ctenitis ampla is a plant species that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. Research suggests that extracts from the plant may have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Additionally, Ctenitis ampla has been investigated for its possible use in treating various health conditions, including fever and gastrointestinal issues. Further studies are needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Ctenitis ampla as a medicinal agent. The plant's traditional uses and potential benefits are still being researched and documented by scientists.

preparations: Ctenitis ampla herbal preparations are traditionally used in Southeast Asian cultures for their medicinal properties, although the specific uses and effectiveness have not been extensively scientifically studied.
